title: Use causal degradation slope and volatility
summary: State-space tyre degradation research highlights latent, time-varying degradation dynamics.
rationale: State-space tyre degradation research highlights latent, time-varying degradation dynamics. Existing features use cumulative degradation and simple ratios, but not causal local slope, curvature, or volatility within the current stint.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint sorted by lap, compute lagged rolling/EWMA features for LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, Deg_Per_TyreLife, and Position_Change: last value, 3-lap slope, acceleration, volatility, and worsening-streak length. Never use centered or future windows.
expected_effect: May improve detection of the lap before a stop, when pace drop-off or instability accelerates before the binary PitStop indicator appears.
risk: Rolling features are fragile with missing laps, pit/out-laps, and extreme lap-time outliers; needs robust clipping and group-boundary handling.
